# Lab-state snapshot

## Context

The rule catalog (cross-prompt pattern #3) identifies server-side
state accumulation as the single largest architectural gap. This
spec introduces `LabStateSnapshot` — a plain-data object that
represents the accumulated state of a chatbot-compile conversation
(deck slots, mounted pipettes, labware on deck, materials in wells,
reservoir contents, labware orientation, mint counter).

Spec-001 created an opaque placeholder `LabStateSnapshot = Record<string, unknown>`.
This spec replaces that placeholder with the real shape and provides
a pure function that folds one `PlateEventPrimitive` into an
existing snapshot.

This spec does NOT thread snapshots through `CompileRequest` /
`CompileResult` (spec-009) and does NOT persist them (spec-010).

## Requirements

1. Create `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts`:

   ```ts
   import type { PlateEventPrimitive } from '../biology/BiologyVerbExpander.js';

   export type LabwareOrientation = 'landscape' | 'portrait';

   export interface DeckSlot {
     slot: string;                  // e.g. 'A1', 'C1', 'D1', 'target'
     labwareInstanceId?: string;    // occupying labware, if any
   }

   export interface MountedPipette {
     mountSide: 'left' | 'right';
     pipetteType: string;           // e.g. '8ch-1000uL', '12ch-300uL'
     maxVolumeUl: number;
   }

   export interface LabwareInstance {
     instanceId: string;
     labwareType: string;           // e.g. '96-well-deepwell-plate'
     slot: string;                  // deck slot occupied
     orientation: LabwareOrientation;
     wells: Record<string, MaterialRecord[]>; // well address -> stack of materials
   }

   export interface MaterialRecord {
     materialId: string;
     kind?: string;                 // e.g. 'fecal-sample', 'binding-buffer'
     volumeUl?: number;
     properties?: Record<string, unknown>;
   }

   export interface ReservoirContents {
     reservoirInstanceId: string;
     wellContents: Record<string, MaterialRecord[]>;
   }

   export interface LabStateSnapshot {
     deck: DeckSlot[];
     mountedPipettes: MountedPipette[];
     labware: Record<string, LabwareInstance>;   // instanceId -> instance
     reservoirs: Record<string, ReservoirContents>;
     mintCounter: number;           // monotonic counter for mint templates
     turnIndex: number;             // incremented per compile
   }

   export function emptyLabState(): LabStateSnapshot {
     return {
       deck: [],
       mountedPipettes: [],
       labware: {},
       reservoirs: {},
       mintCounter: 0,
       turnIndex: 0,
     };
   }

   export function applyEventToLabState(
     snapshot: LabStateSnapshot,
     event: PlateEventPrimitive,
   ): LabStateSnapshot {
     switch (event.event_type) {
       case 'create_container': return applyCreateContainer(snapshot, event);
       case 'add_material':     return applyAddMaterial(snapshot, event);
       case 'transfer':         return applyTransfer(snapshot, event);
       default: return snapshot; // incubate/mix/read/centrifuge have no deck-state effect for now
     }
   }
   ```

   Implement each helper as a pure function that returns a new
   snapshot (use spread copies and `structuredClone` where needed;
   the repo targets Node 20+ where structuredClone is native).

   - `applyCreateContainer`: reads `event.details.slot` and
     `event.details.labwareType`; appends/updates the `deck` entry
     for that slot; creates a new `labware` entry with empty wells
     in `landscape` orientation by default.
   - `applyAddMaterial`: reads `event.details.labwareInstanceId`,
     `event.details.well`, `event.details.material` (object with
     materialId, kind, volumeUl); pushes onto the `wells[well]`
     stack; creates the well key if it didn't exist.
   - `applyTransfer`: reads `event.details.from`, `event.details.to`
     (each `{labwareInstanceId, well}`), `event.details.volumeUl`;
     deducts volume from the top material at `from`, adds a new
     record at `to` referencing the same materialId with the
     transferred volume. If `from` volume is insufficient, produces
     the available amount (no throwing) — mismatch is surfaced by
     validation passes later.

2. Update `server/src/compiler/pipeline/CompileContracts.ts`:
   - Remove the `LabStateSnapshot = Record<string, unknown>` placeholder.
   - Re-export `LabStateSnapshot` from
     `'../state/LabState.js'` so downstream imports continue to
     resolve from `CompileContracts.ts`.

3. Create `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.test.ts` with at
   minimum:

   a) **empty snapshot** — `emptyLabState()` returns the right shape,
      `mintCounter === 0`, `turnIndex === 0`.
   b) **create_container adds deck + labware** — apply a
      create_container event for slot 'target' with labwareType
      '96-well-deepwell-plate'; assert `snapshot.deck` has one entry
      pointing to a new labware instance and
      `snapshot.labware[instanceId]` exists with empty wells.
   c) **add_material adds record** — apply add_material to well A1 of
      the created plate; assert the well list has one record with
      the right materialId.
   d) **transfer moves volume** — apply a transfer of 50uL from well
      A1 to well A2; assert source volume decreased and destination
      has a new record of volumeUl 50.
   e) **immutability** — snapshot the input before apply, apply any
      event, then `expect(input).toEqual(originalCopy)`.

## Files Likely Involved

- `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts` (new)
- `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.test.ts` (new)
- `server/src/compiler/pipeline/CompileContracts.ts` (edit — re-export)

## Verification

```bash

test -f server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts
grep -q 'export function applyEventToLabState' server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts
grep -q 'export function emptyLabState' server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts
grep -q 'export interface LabStateSnapshot' server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts
grep -q "from '../state/LabState" server/src/compiler/pipeline/CompileContracts.ts
pnpm tsc --noEmit
pnpm vitest run server/src/compiler/state/LabState.test.ts
echo OK
```

## Notes

- Do NOT mutate the input snapshot. Use spread / structuredClone /
  Object.freeze if helpful.
- `event.details` is typed as `Record<string, unknown>` in the
  existing `PlateEventPrimitive`. Cast carefully; do not assume
  fields are present without narrowing.
- If an event's `details` are malformed (e.g. missing
  `labwareInstanceId` for add_material), return the snapshot
  unchanged rather than throwing — later validation passes catch
  these cases.

## Local Execution Fit

- This is a narrow local-model slice centered on lab-state snapshot, with a bounded file set and explicit verification.
- Keep the implementation small, preserve existing plumbing, and defer unrelated refactors or broader architecture changes out of scope.

## Non-Negotiable Invariants

- `applyEventToLabState` MUST be a pure function. No global state,
  no console output, no mutation of inputs.
- `LabStateSnapshot` is the single canonical shape. All other
  specs import from this file (or via the CompileContracts re-export).
- No `pnpm test`.

## Dependencies and Tooling

- No new packages unless the requirements explicitly call for one. Use the existing pnpm, TypeScript, and Vitest tooling already in the repo.
- Verification should rely on the exact `pnpm tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm vitest run ...`, and fixture commands named above.

## Golden Examples

- Treat the acceptance criteria for Lab-state snapshot as the canonical success shape; the named files, exported symbols, and fixture outputs should match them exactly.
- When a fixture or registry seed is mentioned, the happy path is that the referenced example loads or runs without adding extra compatibility layers.

## Negative Cases

- Do NOT implement persistence here (spec-010).
- Do NOT wire the snapshot through the compile pipeline here
  (spec-009).
- Do NOT handle directive node types yet — directives come in
  spec-024.
- Do NOT add orientation-change logic here — orientation changes
  are applied by directives, which spec-024 wires into labState.
## Verification Map

- Acceptance criteria about files, exports, and schema shape: verify by inspecting the named files and targeted grep/test checks in the Verification section.
- Acceptance criteria about behavior: verify with the exact typecheck, Vitest, and compile-fixture commands listed above; do not substitute a broader suite.

## Tool Usage Instructions

# Retrieval Tool

The workbench has pre-indexed the target repository using lexical search. Results are injected into the **Retrieval Highlights** section of this prompt.

## How to read results

Each result line follows this format:
```
- <relative_path>:<start_line>-<end_line> score=<rerank_score> reason=<explanation> symbol=<name>
```

- **score**: higher is more relevant (combines lexical match + reranking boosts)
- **reason**: explains why this chunk ranked highly (e.g., "token overlap + path match", "symbol match")
- **symbol**: the function, class, or heading name in that chunk

## How to use retrieval results

1. **Before editing unfamiliar code**, check retrieval results for the relevant file and line range. Read those lines to understand existing patterns before making changes.
2. **Use file:line references** to navigate directly to the code rather than searching from scratch.
3. **Higher-scored results are more likely relevant** to the current task. If the top results point to a file, that file is likely important.
4. **Symbol names** tell you which function or class is in that chunk — use them to understand the codebase structure.
5. **If retrieval returned no matches**, the task may involve new files or areas not well-covered by the index. Explore the repo directory structure instead.

# Playwright MCP Tool

Playwright MCP provides browser automation for testing web UIs. It is available as an MCP server — you can call its tools directly.

## When to use

- The app is running and you need to verify UI behavior
- You need to test user flows (login, form submission, navigation)
- You want visual verification of rendered output

## When NOT to use

- No app is configured or the app is not running
- The task is backend-only with no UI component

## Available tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `browser_navigate` | Navigate to a URL |
| `browser_snapshot` | Get the current page's accessibility snapshot (DOM structure) |
| `browser_click` | Click an element by CSS selector or text |
| `browser_fill` | Fill a form input field |
| `browser_type` | Type text character by character |
| `browser_select_option` | Select from a dropdown |
| `browser_press_key` | Press a keyboard key |
| `browser_take_screenshot` | Capture a screenshot for visual verification |
| `browser_hover` | Hover over an element |
| `browser_wait_for` | Wait for an element or condition |

## Example workflow

1. Find the **App URL** in the **MCP Metadata** section of the prompt (e.g., `http://localhost:5174`)
2. Navigate: `browser_navigate` to that URL
3. Inspect: `browser_snapshot` to see the DOM structure and verify the page rendered
4. Interact: `browser_click` on buttons, `browser_fill` form fields
5. Verify: `browser_snapshot` again to confirm expected state after interaction

## Important

- The Playwright MCP args file listed in MCP Metadata is the launch contract — do not modify it.
- The browser runs headless. Use `browser_snapshot` for DOM inspection rather than relying on visual output.
- If the app healthcheck hasn't passed, Playwright interactions will fail.

## Planner Guidance

# Planner

1. Read the task, repo config, retrieval results, and MCP metadata from the generated loop context.
2. Identify the smallest coherent implementation step that moves the task forward.
3. Choose the minimum file set needed for that step.
4. Decide which repo commands must be run to verify the change.
5. Avoid speculative refactors unless the task requires them.

## Coder Guidance

# Coder

1. Make changes in the target repository only.
2. Prefer the repo-specific commands from the loop context when running checks.
3. Use the retrieval results to confirm file locations and nearby symbols before editing.
4. Keep changes narrow and task-directed.
5. **After making UI changes**: If Playwright MCP is available (status: ready-stdio) and an app is running, you MUST use `browser_navigate` to load the **App URL listed in MCP Metadata above**, then `browser_snapshot` to verify the page renders without errors. Do not skip this step — check the MCP Metadata section for the exact URL.
6. **Before writing API client code**: If OpenAPI MCP is available (spec count > 0), use it to look up the endpoint schema first. Do not guess at request/response shapes.
7. **When stuck on unfamiliar libraries or APIs**: If Exa web search MCP is available, use `web_search_exa` or `get_code_context_exa` to look up documentation and examples.

## Critic Guidance

# Critic

1. Verify the intended behavior with the most relevant available command (tsc, tests, lint).
2. If UI changes were made and Playwright MCP is available, verify the page loads and renders by using `browser_navigate` and `browser_snapshot`.
3. Re-check the task and confirm the edited files actually satisfy each acceptance criterion.
4. Append concrete learnings to the progress log for the next iteration — what was done, what was verified, what's left.
5. If the task cannot be completed safely, leave a precise explanation in the progress log.
6. When ALL criteria are met: output `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` as the absolute last line. Nothing after it.

This\nspec introduces `LabStateSnapshot` \u2014 a plain-data object that\nrepresents the accumulated state of a chatbot-compile conversation\n(deck slots, mounted pipettes, labware on deck, materials in wells,\nreservoir contents, labware orientation, mint counter).\n\nSpec-001 created an opaque placeholder `LabStateSnapshot = Record<string, unknown>`.\nThis spec replaces that placeholder with the real shape and provides\na pure function that folds one `PlateEventPrimitive` into an\nexisting snapshot.\n\nThis spec does NOT thread snapshots through `CompileRequest` /\n`CompileResult` (spec-009) and does NOT persist them (spec-010).\n\n## Requirements\n\n1. Create `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts`:\n\n   ```ts\n   import type { PlateEventPrimitive } from '../biology/BiologyVerbExpander.js';\n\n   export type LabwareOrientation = 'landscape' | 'portrait';\n\n   export interface DeckSlot {\n     slot: string;                  // e.g. 'A1', 'C1', 'D1', 'target'\n     labwareInstanceId?: string;    // occupying labware, if any\n   }\n\n   export interface MountedPipette {\n     mountSide: 'left' | 'right';\n     pipetteType: string;           // e.g. '8ch-1000uL', '12ch-300uL'\n     maxVolumeUl: number;\n   }\n\n   export interface LabwareInstance {\n     instanceId: string;\n     labwareType: string;           // e.g. '96-well-deepwell-plate'\n     slot: string;                  // deck slot occupied\n     orientation: LabwareOrientation;\n     wells: Record<string, MaterialRecord[]>; // well address -> stack of materials\n   }\n\n   export interface MaterialRecord {\n     materialId: string;\n     kind?: string;                 // e.g. 'fecal-sample', 'binding-buffer'\n     volumeUl?: number;\n     properties?: Record<string, unknown>;\n   }\n\n   export interface ReservoirContents {\n     reservoirInstanceId: string;\n     wellContents: Record<string, MaterialRecord[]>;\n   }\n\n   export interface LabStateSnapshot {\n     deck: DeckSlot[];\n     mountedPipettes: MountedPipette[];\n     labware: Record<string, LabwareInstance>;   // instanceId -> instance\n     reservoirs: Record<string, ReservoirContents>;\n     mintCounter: number;           // monotonic counter for mint templates\n     turnIndex: number;             // incremented per compile\n   }\n\n   export function emptyLabState(): LabStateSnapshot {\n     return {\n       deck: [],\n       mountedPipettes: [],\n       labware: {},\n       reservoirs: {},\n       mintCounter: 0,\n       turnIndex: 0,\n     };\n   }\n\n   export function applyEventToLabState(\n     snapshot: LabStateSnapshot,\n     event: PlateEventPrimitive,\n   ): LabStateSnapshot {\n     switch (event.event_type) {\n       case 'create_container': return applyCreateContainer(snapshot, event);\n       case 'add_material':     return applyAddMaterial(snapshot, event);\n       case 'transfer':         return applyTransfer(snapshot, event);\n       default: return snapshot; // incubate/mix/read/centrifuge have no deck-state effect for now\n     }\n   }\n   ```\n\n   Implement each helper as a pure function that returns a new\n   snapshot (use spread copies and `structuredClone` where needed;\n   the repo targets Node 20+ where structuredClone is native).\n\n   - `applyCreateContainer`: reads `event.details.slot` and\n     `event.details.labwareType`; appends/updates the `deck` entry\n     for that slot; creates a new `labware` entry with empty wells\n     in `landscape` orientation by default.\n   - `applyAddMaterial`: reads `event.details.labwareInstanceId`,\n     `event.details.well`, `event.details.material` (object with\n     materialId, kind, volumeUl); pushes onto the `wells[well]`\n     stack; creates the well key if it didn't exist.\n   - `applyTransfer`: reads `event.details.from`, `event.details.to`\n     (each `{labwareInstanceId, well}`), `event.details.volumeUl`;\n     deducts volume from the top material at `from`, adds a new\n     record at `to` referencing the same materialId with the\n     transferred volume. If `from` volume is insufficient, produces\n     the available amount (no throwing) \u2014 mismatch is surfaced by\n     validation passes later.\n\n2. Update `server/src/compiler/pipeline/CompileContracts.ts`:\n   - Remove the `LabStateSnapshot = Record<string, unknown>` placeholder.\n   - Re-export `LabStateSnapshot` from\n     `'../state/LabState.js'` so downstream imports continue to\n     resolve from `CompileContracts.ts`.\n\n3. Create `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.test.ts` with at\n   minimum:\n\n   a) **empty snapshot** \u2014 `emptyLabState()` returns the right shape,\n      `mintCounter === 0`, `turnIndex === 0`.\n   b) **create_container adds deck + labware** \u2014 apply a\n      create_container event for slot 'target' with labwareType\n      '96-well-deepwell-plate'; assert `snapshot.deck` has one entry\n      pointing to a new labware instance and\n      `snapshot.labware[instanceId]` exists with empty wells.\n   c) **add_material adds record** \u2014 apply add_material to well A1 of\n      the created plate; assert the well list has one record with\n      the right materialId.\n   d) **transfer moves volume** \u2014 apply a transfer of 50uL from well\n      A1 to well A2; assert source volume decreased and destination\n      has a new record of volumeUl 50.\n   e) **immutability** \u2014 snapshot the input before apply, apply any\n      event, then `expect(input).toEqual(originalCopy)`.\n\n## Files Likely Involved\n\n- `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts` (new)\n- `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.test.ts` (new)\n- `server/src/compiler/pipeline/CompileContracts.ts` (edit \u2014 re-export)\n\n## Verification\n\n```bash\ncd /home/brad/git/computable-lab\ntest -f server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts\ngrep -q 'export function applyEventToLabState' server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts\ngrep -q 'export function emptyLabState' server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts\ngrep -q 'export interface LabStateSnapshot' server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts\ngrep -q \"from '../state/LabState\" server/src/compiler/pipeline/CompileContracts.ts\npnpm tsc --noEmit\npnpm vitest run server/src/compiler/state/LabState.test.ts\necho OK\n```\n\n## Notes\n\n- Do NOT mutate the input snapshot. Use spread / structuredClone /\n  Object.freeze if helpful.\n- `event.details` is typed as `Record<string, unknown>` in the\n  existing `PlateEventPrimitive`. Cast carefully; do not assume\n  fields are present without narrowing.\n- If an event's `details` are malformed (e.g. missing\n  `labwareInstanceId` for add_material), return the snapshot\n  unchanged rather than throwing \u2014 later validation passes catch\n  these cases.\n\n## Local Execution Fit\n\n- This is a narrow local-model slice centered on lab-state snapshot, with a bounded file set and explicit verification.\n- Keep the implementation small, preserve existing plumbing, and defer unrelated refactors or broader architecture changes out of scope.\n\n## Non-Negotiable Invariants\n\n- `applyEventToLabState` MUST be a pure function. No global state,\n  no console output, no mutation of inputs.\n- `LabStateSnapshot` is the single canonical shape. All other\n  specs import from this file (or via the CompileContracts re-export).\n- No `pnpm test`.\n\n## Dependencies and Tooling\n\n- No new packages unless the requirements explicitly call for one. Use the existing pnpm, TypeScript, and Vitest tooling already in the repo.\n- Verification should rely on the exact `pnpm tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm vitest run ...`, and fixture commands named above.\n\n## Golden Examples\n\n- Treat the acceptance criteria for Lab-state snapshot as the canonical success shape; the named files, exported symbols, and fixture outputs should match them exactly.\n- When a fixture or registry seed is mentioned, the happy path is that the referenced example loads or runs without adding extra compatibility layers.\n\n## Negative Cases\n\n- Do NOT implement persistence here (spec-010).\n- Do NOT wire the snapshot through the compile pipeline here\n  (spec-009).\n- Do NOT handle directive node types yet \u2014 directives come in\n  spec-024.\n- Do NOT add orientation-change logic here \u2014 orientation changes\n  are applied by directives, which spec-024 wires into labState.\n## Verification Map\n\n- Acceptance criteria about files, exports, and schema shape: verify by inspecting the named files and targeted grep/test checks in the Verification section.\n- Acceptance criteria about behavior: verify with the exact typecheck, Vitest, and compile-fixture commands listed above; do not substitute a broader suite.\n\n## Progress Log\n\n### 2026-04-24T19:44:00Z - Iteration 1: Complete\n\nAll acceptance criteria met:\n\n1. **Created `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.ts`** with:\n   - `LabwareOrientation` type (`'landscape' | 'portrait'`)\n   - `DeckSlot`, `MountedPipette`, `LabwareInstance`, `MaterialRecord`, `ReservoirContents`, `LabStateSnapshot` interfaces\n   - `emptyLabState()` factory returning correct initial shape\n   - `applyEventToLabState()` pure function handling `create_container`, `add_material`, `transfer` event types\n   - Each handler uses `structuredClone` for immutability\n   - Malformed events return snapshot unchanged (no throwing)\n\n2. **Updated `server/src/compiler/pipeline/CompileContracts.ts`**:\n   - Removed opaque `LabStateSnapshot = Record<string, unknown>` placeholder\n   - Added `export type { LabStateSnapshot } from '../state/LabState.js'` re-export\n\n3. **Created `server/src/compiler/state/LabState.test.ts`** with 5 tests:\n   - empty snapshot shape (mintCounter=0, turnIndex=0)\n   - create_container adds deck entry + labware instance\n   - add_material adds record to well\n   - transfer moves volume from source to destination\n   - immutability (input snapshot unchanged after apply)\n\n4. **Verification passed**:\n   - `pnpm tsc --noEmit` \u2014 clean\n   - `pnpm vitest run server/src/compiler/state/LabState.test.ts` \u2014 5/5 passed\n\n\n\n---\n\n## Prior Critic Feedback (from local coder attempts)\n\nA local coding model attempted this spec and was rejected by the critic.\nAddress every item below.\n\n### Critic Verdict: PASS\n\n**Summary**: LabState.ts implements all required types, emptyLabState factory, and pure applyEventToLabState function. CompileContracts.ts properly imports and re-exports LabStateSnapshot. All 5 tests pass. ## Delivery Rules

1. Focus on one story only.
2. Use retrieval evidence before editing unfamiliar areas.
3. Prefer repo-configured lint, test, and build commands when verifying changes.
4. If you use Playwright or OpenAPI MCP, treat the generated args/config files as the launch contract.
5. **After any UI change, you MUST verify in the browser.** Use `browser_navigate` to load the App URL from MCP Metadata above, then `browser_snapshot` to confirm the page renders. If it shows errors, fix them before marking the story complete.
6. **Tool-usage criteria are enforced.** Acceptance criteria may explicitly require a specific tool call (e.g. *"at least one `browser_snapshot` against the rendered page"*). The critic sees a per-tool count from your trace and will auto-reject if a required tool shows zero calls — tsc passing is not enough. Read every criterion and make sure you have actually used the tools each one demands before emitting the completion promise.
7. When the story is complete, update the progress log file at `/home/brad/git/agent-workbench/state/sessions/computable-lab/2026-04-24-compile-pipeline-universals/specs/.escalation-spec-008-lab-state-snapshot-mpmeT7.md` with what you did.
8. If the task is blocked, record the exact blocker and stop cleanly.
9. **CRITICAL — Completion signal**: When and ONLY when ALL acceptance criteria are met, output `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` as the **very last line** of your response. Nothing may follow it — no summary, no explanation, no whitespace. If ralph does not detect this exact tag as your final line, the loop will continue unnecessarily.
